title: SIR Epidemic Parameter Estimation
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is a widefield-fluorescence fan-out artifact; principle grounded in domain literature.

E.description: SIR Epidemic Parameter Estimation: SIR model fitting: estimate transmission rate beta and recovery rate gamma from epidemic incidence data. The forward operator produces the measurement through a 3-node primitive DAG (M.ode.sir_model…); recovery is posed as a parameter_estimation problem. Difficulty tier delta=3 with effective condition number kappa_eff~50; under_reporting_factor, heterogeneous_mixing set the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form equation.
E.forward_model: dS/dt = -beta*S*I/N; dI/dt = beta*S*I/N - gamma*I; dR/dt = gamma*I; R0 = beta/gamma
E.world_state_x: SIR parameters (beta_transmission, gamma_recovery) and R0 basic reproduction number
E.observation_y: daily/weekly incidence data I_new(t) = beta*S*I/N for epidemic period

W.regime: Existence of the recovered SIR_parameter_vector is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by declared priors. Stability is conditionally stable (kappa_eff ~= 50); under_reporting_factor dominates the stability cliff; the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Negative binomial sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor.

C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: R0 uncertainty < 0.2 for well-detected epidemic. Noise floor: O(sigma / sqrt(kappa_eff)) with kappa_eff~50. Mismatch-induced bias + C2 * |under_reporting_factor|; iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the optimum; epsilon_fn captures quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(N_days * N_MCMC) per epidemic fit per iteration
